n, k = map(int, input().split())
denominations = list(map(int, input().split()))
q = int(input())
for _ in range(q):
    x = int(input())
    # If the maximum denomination is greater than x, then we cannot get x
    if denominations[-1] > x:
        print(-1)
        continue
    # Initialize the count to 0
    count = 0
    # Iterate through the denominations
    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        # If the current denomination is greater than x, then move to the next denomination
        if denominations[i] > x:
            continue
        # If the current denomination is equal to x, then add 1 to the count and break out of the loop
        if denominations[i] == x:
            count += 1
            break
        # If the current denomination is less than x, then add the number of times we can use the current denomination to get to x and add the number of times we can use the next denomination to get to the remainder
        count += (x // denominations[i]) + (x % denominations[i])
